card.intuition: A separate data structure (usually a B-Tree) that improves the speed of data retrieval operations at the cost of slower writes and extra storage.
card.analogy: The index at the back of a textbook: instead of reading through the entire 500-page book to find 'DNS', you look up 'DNS' in the index and jump straight to page 342.
card.time_complexity: O(log n) for searching indexed columns; O(n) for non-indexed (Full Table Scan)
when_to_use: Columns used frequently in WHERE clauses
Columns used in ORDER BY and GROUP BY clauses to speed up sorting
gotchas: Over-indexing' slows down INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE operations because the database must update every index for every change
Indices are not automatically used for LIKE queries starting with a wildcard (e.g., '%search') — this still triggers a full table scan

exercise.prompt: Complete the SQL command to create an index on the 'email' column of the 'users' table.
hints: You create the index 'ON' a specific table
